stemHtml: The Commander of a commercial air transport IFR flight receives an updated in-flight weather report. After in-flight fuel check, he/she determines that the fuel expected to remain upon arrival at the destination is less than the Final Reserve Fuel (FRF). A destination alternate aerodrome is not required for this flight and the Basic Fuel Scheme without variations is applied. Which action should be taken by the Commander?

options.a: Replan the fuel and apply a Reduced Contingency Fuel procedure, in order to continue to the destination aerodrome.
options.b: Continue to the destination aerodrome as planned by using the FRF if it is sufficient to reach there.
options.c: Divert to an aerodrome where a safe landing can be assured with NO less than the FRF.
